worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: Clearest first-look breakdown of DeepSeek V4 benchmarks — SOTA open-source coding at frontier prices, with the right caveat that real-world testing against GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.7 is still needed.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon's notes confirm DeepSeek V4-Flash and V4-Pro are the cheapest models in their tiers while benchmarking near frontier — the pricing angle is the actual story for anyone building on top of models.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: GPT-5.5 pricing has gone 8x in 8 months ($0.63 → $5.00 per million input tokens) — a concrete data point for anyone thinking about AI product cost structure and margin.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Box ran real enterprise evals on GPT-5.5 over two weeks and saw a 10-point accuracy jump on knowledge work tasks vs. GPT-5.4 — one of the few practitioner signals rather than benchmark theater.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: Codex now ships an auto-review guardian sub-agent that gates unsafe actions, reducing human approvals — this is the concrete agent oversight pattern that will define production agentic systems.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Sharp argument that Tripadvisor, Instacart, Spotify etc. are structurally exposed as AI agents route around them — directly relevant to distribution strategy for anyone building indie or product.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Concise articulation of why AI amplifies rather than flattens talent: the bottleneck has shifted from 'can you build it' to taste and judgment — worth 30 seconds for anyone hiring or positioning.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The 'app as noun' framing — iPhone-era apps as typed buffers — is a tight lens on where the next product paradigm is heading, relevant to agent-native product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @businessbarista
worthClicking.why: Firsthand account of running an AI literacy session for 20 financial-institution execs — the aha moments listed are a useful read on where enterprise adoption actually is right now.

voices.why: Fastest and most accurate real-time coverage of model releases with appropriate epistemic caveats — best signal on DeepSeek V4 and model competition this cycle.
Practitioner-level notes on new models (DeepSeek V4 pricing, LLM tooling) with actual hands-on testing rather than benchmark parroting.
Consistently sharp on product paradigm shifts (app-as-noun, talent amplification, aesthetic convergence) — worth reading for AI product strategy framing.
Strong on GPT-5.5 pricing math and aggregator disruption thesis, though signal is diluted by high-volume newsletter-promo spam.
One of few enterprise operators sharing actual internal eval results on frontier models — rare practitioner voice on enterprise AI adoption.

examples: @aakashgupta newsletter self-promotion posted 4+ times with zero content
RoundtableSpace all-caps hype posts with no original analysis (GM posts, trading bot claims, Boomers vs AI memes)
JaynitMakwana threadboi promotional thread for Cappy/Mirage video editor with sub-200 view tweets

fading.theme: AI product strategy for solo founders & UGC distribution
Anthropic PR silence on pricing blunder
